propositions: Removed env vars are silently ignored and fall through to defaults with no warning, and removed TOML keys get only a generic unknown-key warning that names no replacement.
A retired store/security/policy/sync input must raise a fail-loud RemovedInputError naming old name, replacement, and removed-in version, with a nonzero exit and no store mutation or network call.
RemovedInputError must not subclass ConfigError so tracker_dir()'s except ConfigError does not swallow it and silently redirect the store to .tickets-tracker.

The env-var tombstone check must run at the top of load_config() before the _RESULT_CACHE lookup and add tombstoned names to _env_signature() so it fires even on a warm cache hit in the long-running MCP server.
Operationally-inert retired inputs should warn rather than error while the process continues to normal completion.
A leftover flat .rebar/config.conf file must be detected as a file-presence tombstone at discovery, and genuinely-unknown keys must retain the existing forward-compat WARN policy.
